note: Image-provenance task. The executor is given ONLY a cropped interior detail of a painting (sources/crop.jpg) with no title, signature, frame, or caption. They must visually identify the artwork and trace it to its canonical museum record. Searching text cannot short-circuit this — the crop carries no searchable text. Ground truth is the Met Collection API record.

crop_origin.source_full_image: Met Collection API primaryImage for objectID 436535 (https://images.metmuseum.org/CRDImages/ep/original/DP-42549-001.jpg)
crop_origin.what_it_shows: an interior detail: van Gogh's swirling impasto sky and a cypress/foliage band; no signature or frame edges

canonical_answer.holding_institution: The Metropolitan Museum of Art (The Met)

title: Wheat Field with Cypresses
A Wheatfield, with Cypresses
Wheatfield with Cypresses
accepted_variants.note: Van Gogh painted several closely related 'Wheat Field with Cypresses' versions (Met 1889; National Gallery London; others). The Met version (objectID 436535, accession 1993.132) is the one this crop is taken from. Identifying the artwork + artist correctly is the core; landing specifically on the Met record (object id / accession / Met URL) is required for full credit because the crop is pixel-derived from the Met image.

scoring_anchors.primary_source_rule: the canonical answer is the museum's own record (Met page or Met Collection API). Wikipedia/WikiArt/blog reproductions are secondary — acceptable as supporting evidence but the final canonical pointer should be the Met record.
scoring_anchors.anti_secondary_source: stopping at a Wikipedia/Pinterest/stock-photo page without reaching the Met (or at least the holding institution's own record) caps the score
scoring_anchors.tolerance: If the executor identifies the artwork + artist correctly and reaches a van Gogh 'Wheat Field with Cypresses' museum record but names a sibling version (e.g. National Gallery) instead of the Met, give partial credit (it is the right artwork family but the wrong specific holding for this exact image).
